zh.Mallorca, Illes Balears, Spain: 西班牙易也斯 巴萊亞爾斯馬洛爾卡
zh.lifeSummary: 當Guillaume II de Béarn於1194年9月12日出生在西班牙易也斯 巴萊亞爾斯馬洛爾卡時，他的父親Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn28歲，母親Guilleuma de Castellvell51歲。他於1220年與Gersende De Provence De Sabran Y Folcaquier在西班牙卡塔倫亞赫羅納艾爾 吉洛斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1229年9月12日享年35歲，埋葬在西班牙卡塔倫亞巴薩隆拿。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Guillaume II de Béarn于1194年9月12日出生在西班牙易也斯 巴萊亞爾斯馬洛爾卡时，他的父亲Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn28岁，母亲Guilleuma de Castellvell51岁。他于1220年与Gersende De Provence De Sabran Y Folcaquier在西班牙卡塔倫亞赫羅納艾爾 吉洛斯结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1229年9月12日享年35岁，埋葬在西班牙卡塔倫亞巴薩隆拿。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Béarn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: possibly a shortened and altered form of Scottish   McLaren  .

variants.name: McLaren
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Labhrainn ‘son of Labhrann’, Gaelic form of the personal name Lawrence .

History: This is the name of a Scottish clan that appears to have two distinct origins. One group traces its ancestry to Laurence (Gaelic Labhrann), Abbot of Balquhidder in Perths in the 13th century. The other group claims a historical association with the island of Tiree in the Hebrides.

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Guillaume II de Béarn was born on 12 September 1194, in Mallorca, Illes Balears, Spain, his father, Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn, was 28 and his mother, Guilleuma de Castellvell, was 51. He married Gersende De Provence De Sabran Y Folcaquier in 1220, in el Gironès, Girona, Catalonia, Spain. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 12 September 1229, at the age of 35, and was buried in Barcelona, Catalonia, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Guillaume II de Béarn est né le 12 septembre 1194, à Mallorca, Illes Balears, Espagne, son père, Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Guilleuma de Castellvell, avait 51 ans. Il a épousé Gersende De Provence De Sabran Y Folcaquier en 1220, à el Gironès, Girona, Catalunya, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 12 septembre 1229, à l’âge de 35 ans, et a été enterré à Barcelona, Catalunya, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Guillaume II de Béarn am 12. September 1194 in Mallorca, Illes Balears, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn 28 und seine Mutter Guilleuma de Castellvell 51. Er hat 1220 in el Gironès, Girona, Catalunya, Spanien Gersende De Provence De Sabran Y Folcaquier geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 12. September 1229 im Alter von 35 Jahren und wurde in Barcelona, Catalunya, Spanien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Guillaume II de Béarn è nato il il 12 settembre 1194 nel Mallorca, Illes Balears, Spagna, il suo padre, Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Guilleuma de Castellvell, aveva 51 anni. Lui sposato Gersende De Provence De Sabran Y Folcaquier nel 1220, nel el Gironès, Girona, Catalunya, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 12 settembre 1229, all'età di 35 anni, e fu sepolto a Barcelona, Catalunya, Spagna.

ja.lifeSummary: Guillaume II de Béarnが1194年9月12日にスペインイレス ボーエアルズマロルカで生まれた時，父Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearnは28歳，母Guilleuma de Castellvellは51歳であった。1220年にスペインカタルニーアギロナエル ギロンeスでGersende De Provence De Sabran Y Folcaquierと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1229年9月12日に35歳で死去し，スペインカタルニーアバーセロナにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Guillaume II de Béarn(이)가 1194년 9월 12일 스페인 이레스 베리르스 맬로르카에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Guilleuma de Castellvell(은)는 51세였다. 그는 1220년 스페인 카털니아 지로나 엘 지론e스에서 Gersende De Provence De Sabran Y Folcaquier(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1229년 9월 12일 35세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 카털니아 바셀나에 묻혔다.

mn.12/9/1194–12/9/1229: 1194 оны Есдүгээр сарын 12–1229 оны Есдүгээр сарын 12

pt.12/9/1194–12/9/1229: 12 de setembro de 1194–12 de setembro de 1229
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Guillaume II de Béarn nasceu em 12 de setembro de 1194, em Mallorca, Illes Balears, Espanha, seu pai, Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Guilleuma de Castellvell, tinha 51 anos. Ele casou-se com Gersende De Provence De Sabran Y Folcaquier em 1220, em el Gironès, Girona, Catalunya, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 12 de setembro de 1229, com 35 anos, e foi sepultado em Barcelona, Catalunya, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Guillaume II de Béarn родился 12 сентября 1194 г. в местности маллорка, иллесь беилирс, Испания, его отцу по имени Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Guilleuma de Castellvell, 51. Он женился на женщине по имени Gersende De Provence De Sabran Y Folcaquier в 1220 г. в местности эл гиронeс, гирона, каталуня, Испания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 12 сентября 1229 г. в возрасте 35 лет и был похоронен в местности барселона, каталуня, Испания.

es.12/9/1194–12/9/1229: 12 de septiembre de 1194–12 de septiembre de 1229
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Guillaume II de Béarn nació el 12 de septiembre de 1194, en Mallorca, Islas Baleares, España, su papá, Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Guilleuma de Castellvell, tenía 51 años. Se casó con Gersende De Provence De Sabran Y Folcaquier en 1220, en el Gironès, Gerona, Cataluña, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 12 de septiembre de 1229, a la edad de 35 años, y fue sepultado en Barcelona, Cataluña, España.
